name: chromatin_accessibility_beam_search
description: De novo design of a 16,384 bp DNA region whose central 1,024 bp interval has high predicted DNase-seq accessibility in K562 cells (`EFO:0002067`, ENCODE/Roadmap Epigenomics reference erythroleukemia line). Generation uses the `beam-search` optimizer driving Evo2-7B (Nguyen et al. 2024) with KV-cache reuse: a 16 bp prompt seeds the autoregressive generation but is *not* prepended to the output (`prepend_prompt: false` in both the optimizer and the generator configs, so the output segment length is exactly 8 * 2048 = 16384 = AlphaGenome's smallest supported context). At each beam boundary the optimizer keeps the top `num_results=2` continuations by score. The `alphagenome-interval-track` constraint (Avsec et al. 2024 `AlphaGenome` model, on-device with `requested_output=DNASE`, `direction=maximize`, `intervals=[(7680, 8704)]`) calls AlphaGenome on each candidate and scores the DNase track signal averaged over the central 1 kb. AlphaGenome requires the input length to be exactly one of (16384, 131072, 524288, 1048576) - we use the smallest. Cell-type ontology drives K562; swap `ontology_terms` to e.g. `["EFO:0002824"]` (HepG2) or `["EFO:0001086"]` (GM12878) to retarget.
